annual_lag1.anomalies: complete annual total minus the record mean
annual_lag1.bias_correction: Quenouille first-order correction r + (1 + 3r) / n, clipped to the open correlation interval

availability: apply every objective-registry minimum-support rule to each registered month or season; an objective is station-available only when all mandatory cells are available, and a mandatory stratum requires two station-available contributors
distance_weighting: where the A9a registry says weighted but supplies no numeric weights, use equal component weights fixed before outcome access; correlation distances use Fisher z and scale floors are the registry absolute floors
extremes: for each 1, 3, and 5 day annual-maximum series report empirical median and q90 plus sample L1, L2, and L-skewness; no MLE GEV fit and no extrapolated return level enters pass/fail
family_gate: retain the A9c candidate-blind paired year-block family summaries and fresh A9c3 thresholds as the familywise materiality layer; the 27 scientific objective rows independently enforce registered estimators, support, availability, and distances and cannot be converted to favorable zeros
seasonal_adjacency: adjacent-wet pairs require consecutive civil dates and membership of both days in the same registered season; dry gaps are counted on continuous chronology and assigned to the season of the terminating wet day
temperature: for each DJF/MAM/JJA/SON cell compute wet-minus-dry Tmax mean, Tmax sample SD, Tmin mean, and Tmin sample SD with at least 100 wet and 100 dry valid days
winter: a winter is December assigned to the following civil year plus that year's January and February; retain only complete three-month winters, use mean temperature <= 0 C for the cold fraction, and count only transitions across consecutive valid civil days
